The main unit of analysis is the community. Communities are typically small and rural, with an average population size of 3,793 inhabitants; there are 848 communities in the sample. Vote shares at the community level are based on official election results as documented by the Central Election Commission. The main explanatory variables are the prevalence of emigration to the West and East, which are measured as the share of westward and eastward migrants in the total population of each community (in percent).  The dataset contains: -migration patterns and results of the July 2009 parliamentary election -migration patterns and communist votes over time, 2001–2010 -a binary indicator for whether a communist mayor was elected in the municipal elections of 1999, 2003, and 2007 -individual political preferences over time, 2002–2009 -estimates for 8,350 individuals using data from several rounds of the Moldovan Political Barometer (the sample is based on a pooled cross-section of all rounds conducted between April 2002 and March 2009) -individual-level migration patterns and communist votes in 2010 (estimates for 7,344 individuals using data from an exit poll conducted during the parliamentary election of November 2010) Materials were downloaded and uploaded to the project's OSF on 23 December 2021",na,"sample_size, coefficient","Sample size

The data is a record of trade relations between WTO members in disputed products. The unit of observation is dispute-trade partner-year, where ‘trade partner’ refers to all complainants, third parties and non-participants that trade with the defendant. The dataset only includes disputes for which there exists a complete record of trade data (242 disputes, 260 complainants, 650 third parties and 6,800 non-participants). The data was accessed and downloaded on 27 January 2022 and uploaded to the OSF on the same day.",less-than,"sample_size, p_value, coefficient","Sample size

column1: 2022-02-02T09:18:12Z,Antràs_Econometrica_2013_a2Yx - Szabelska - 28m96,Priya,Push Button Reproduction,https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/16U9JQ-keYMHQ5rF7rFv7toKpobuEytFAFlAfwgWSisM/edit#gid=47143620,https://osf.io/qk9v3/?view_only=3529ae21e0314e029e9b75ab66381ba0,3lxxq3,https://scholar.harvard.edu/antras/publications/organizing-global-value-chain,"The dataset is composed of data from: (1) the U.S. Census Bureau’s Related Party Trade database which reports U.S. trade volumes at the detailed country-industry, (2) estimates of U.S. import demand elasticities from Broda and Weinstein’s Globalization and the Gains From Variety (2006), and (3) U.S. Input–Output Tables. column1: 2022-03-29T09:19:31Z,Gerber_BritJournPoliSci_2018_3WmY - Pavlović - 675ko,Priya,Push Button Reproduction,https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1rAU7nsedigE5W9edoWYYWt_WK4p_sJtG_dE-fPmK8A0/edit#gid=226716288,https://osf.io/hyqpr/?view_only=badda9e14c0f4995aad2ccace1d3f651,single-trace,https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/XQTCHB,"The claim that needs to be reproduced relates to the data in Table 2 in the paper (p. 1112); the full model is also shown in the online appendix, Table A11 (available at https://static.cambridge.org/content/id/urn:cambridge.org:id:article:S0007123416000144/resource/name/S0007123416000144sup001.pdf, accessed 3/23/2022). The data needed for the reproduction were available as part of the original materials and include dataset ""SANDERS_replication.dta"" and its accompanying do-file, ""Replicate Models in Table 1 and 2.do"". The dataset includes 36 variables and 4,384 observations. Variables cover measures of the main dependent variables, immigration position and change in immigration position between two waves of survey, several individual level predictors (age, gender, left-right ideology etc.) and one group level predictor. Their labels and descriptions are given in the codebook (pp. 2-3) which is part of the original materials (https://dataverse.harvard.edu/file.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/XQTCHB/WGZTFT&version=1.0 accessed at 3/23/2022). The do-file is commented, with clear indication which commands relate to which data presented in the paper. This dataset and do-file enable a simple push-button reproduction of the data presented in Table 2 in the paper which directly relate to this claim 4 and focal variable coefficients mentioned in it (the sample size can be reproduced as well since it is shown in the ""N individuals"" row Table 2 in the paper, although not mentioned in claim 4).",less-than,"sample_size, p_value, coefficient","Sample size

column1: 2022-03-29T12:06:50Z,Bigoni_Econometrica_2015_VBx1 - Pavlović - 6m31g,Priya,Push Button Reproduction,https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1OEdxfuXOuycv8GpedTGoyZrTeg2yqJRTSCbykc-RacI/edit#gid=226716288,https://osf.io/5fc4n/?view_only=d534939467f94a86bde80caef809ea59,single-trace,https://www.econometricsociety.org/content/supplement-time-horizon-and-cooperation-continuous-time-0,"Data needed for the reproduction were part of the original materials, a number of files in various format described in more details in the ReadMe.pdf file, available at https://www.econometricsociety.org/content/supplement-time-horizon-and-cooperation-continuous-time-0, accessed 03/24/2022). They include three STATA datasets ""data.dta"", ""globals.dta"" and ""graphs.dta"" (as described in the ReadMe file, the second and the third are used for purposes that go beyond the scope of this reproduction). Analysis should be carried out by running ""data_analysis.do"" do-file which requires the ancillary file ""prepare_dataset.do"", which is also available. The main dataset, data.dta, includes 72 variables and 6,936 observations. Do-file ""data_analysis"" runs all of the analyses presented in the Paper (full model has been reported in the Supplementary material, TABLE B.II, available at https://www.econometricsociety.org/content/supplement-time-horizon-and-cooperation-continuous-time), and, as described, produces two additional datasets, ""graphs_data_ready.dta"" and ""subjects_data_ready.dta"", which are already part of the original materials. The ""subject_data ready.dta"" dataset and ""data_analysis.do"" do-file enable a simple push-button reproduction of the data presented in Table III in the paper (p. 596) which directly relate to this claim 4 and focal variable coefficients mentioned in it (the sample size can be reproduced as well since it is shown in Table III in the paper, although not mentioned in claim 4).",less-than,"sample_size, p_value, coefficient","Sample size

The datasets DS0-DS3 are available for download and the datasets DS4-DS13 are avaiable for online analysis.The datasets DS0-DS3 were downloaded by the researcher on March 30, 2022 and uploaded to the OSF restricted-access folder titled ICPSR_04075 on the same day. The data are allowed for individual research purposes, but are not allowed for public redistribution. The researcher requested ICPSR for the access to the datasets DS4-DS13 on March 22, 2022. ""The Early Childhood Longitudinal Study, Kindergarten Class of 1998-1999 (ECLS-K) focuses on children's early school experiences beginning with kindergarten through fifth grade. It is a nationally representative sample that collects information from children, their families, their teachers, and their schools. ECLS-K provides data about the effects of a wide range of family, school, community, and individual variables on children's development, early learning, and early performance in school. This data collection contains the wave of data collected in the spring of third grade (2002). The third-grade data collection includes information about the diversity of the study children, the schools they attended, and their academic progress in the years following kindergarten. Other variables include child gender, child race, family background, childcare, childcare arrangements, food security, hours per week in child care, socioeconomic status, household income, highest level of education for parents and students, parents' employment status, teachers' evaluation practice, and usefulness of different activities in the classroom."" Citation: United States Department of Education. National Center for Education Statistics. Early Childhood Longitudinal Study [United States]: Kindergarten Class of 1998-1999, Third Grade. Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research [distributor], 2013-08-12. https://doi.org/10.3886/ICPSR04075.v1",exact,"sample_size, p_value, coefficient","Sample size

I think there are two ways to achieve reproduction success: precise reproduction is achieved if all defined primary and secondary outcomes (i.e. sample size, focal variable coefficient and significance of the focal variable coefficient) meet the precise reproduction criteria, while approximate reproduction is achieved if some or all the outcomes meet only the approximate criteria and none of the outcomes is in the 'Not reproduced' criteria. column1: 2022-04-21T06:06:12Z,Robinson_EduRes_2010_g0XQ - Kačmár - 6owm3,Priya,Source Data Reproduction,https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1AzQ9YG2MC_LEEg-bExLGR-r2eYaSaGUESSYPrdAHNs0/edit#gid=226716288,https://osf.io/drcnw/?view_only=423bffa36d84438a8f950b26c5af5b65,single-trace,https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/web/ICPSR/studies/28023#,"As noted by Robinson (2010; p. 583): The ECLS-K mathematics assessments were created by the Educational Testing Service (ETS) based on existing and ommonly used commercial assessments, including the Test of Early Mathematics Ability–Third Edition, the WoodcockJohnson III, and the Peabody Individual Achievement Test–Revised. As more version od data are available, dataset DS1: ECLS-K Kindergarten Eighth Grade Full Sample Public-Use File has been selected as it ""Includes the kindergarten, first, third, fifth, and eighth grade data collections for the Early Childhood Longitudinal Study, Kindergarten Class of 1998-1999 (ECLS-K). ECLS-K has been assessed via link above (it is necessary to register to download the data). Downloaded file ""contains all data for all ECLS-K sample cases that have been publicly released in any of the rounds. It focuses on children's early school experiences."" Data were downloaded 11.02.2022. With regards to specific variables, based on Robinson (2010) and codebook, child´s total OLDS score (SCTOT); Math score (MTSC); Child composite race (RACE), and weight (full sample) (CW0) has been selected as variables of interest for the second wave of data collection (round 2 - Kindergarten, Spring 1999). column1: 2022-05-16T06:04:51Z,Hofer_LearnInst_2012_rWbG - Ropovik - 6ow93,Priya,Author Data Reproduction,https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Uw7xro4imGAHZ5DI-YetnS4kPPP4_9QP3u_5mpctEJA/edit#gid=1588206642,https://osf.io/zw4xy/?view_only=6898559ecd58443d907b24fee717be68,single-trace,https://osf.io/yc3jh/?view_only=e1f712efc64b4c128599320ee28c96d7,"Although for this given claim, neither original authors' raw data, nor source data were available, it was possible to reconstruct the variance-covariance matrix that represent the input for the modeling of latent structures. The data for this given analysis is thus represented by the variance-covariance matrix which, in turn, was reproduced using the reported zero-order correlation matrix and set of SDs for the model variables. The absence of raw data does not matter for the estimation of the model parameters because any SEM analysis using maximum likelihood estimation only simply compiles the variance-covariance matrix and further works only with that. Specifically, the data representing the input for the modeling of covariance structures were obtained by coding the matrix of zero-order correlations (Table 3) and SDs (Table 3) of the model variables and computing the variance-covariance matrix using the cor2cov() function from the lavaan R package. In the data file, the off-diagonal matrix elements represent the zero-order correlations, the diagonal values are the SDs.",less-than,"sample_size, p_value, test_statistic","Sample size

column1: 2022-05-19T20:42:21Z,Hofer_LearnInst_2012_rWbG - Ropovik - 6ow93,Zach,Author Data Reproduction,https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Uw7xro4imGAHZ5DI-YetnS4kPPP4_9QP3u_5mpctEJA/edit#gid=1588206642,https://osf.io/zw4xy/?view_only=6898559ecd58443d907b24fee717be68,single-trace,https://osf.io/yc3jh/?view_only=e1f712efc64b4c128599320ee28c96d7,"Although for this given claim, neither original authors' raw data, nor source data were available, it was possible to reconstruct the variance-covariance matrix that represent the input for the modeling of latent structures. The data for this given analysis is thus represented by the variance-covariance matrix which, in turn, was reproduced using the reported zero-order correlation matrix and set of SDs for the model variables. The absence of raw data does not matter for the estimation of the model parameters because any SEM analysis using maximum likelihood estimation only simply compiles the variance-covariance matrix and further works only with that. Specifically, the data representing the input for the modeling of covariance structures were obtained by coding the matrix of zero-order correlations (Table 3) and SDs (Table 3) of the model variables and computing the variance-covariance matrix using the cor2cov() function from the lavaan R package. In the data file, the off-diagonal matrix elements represent the zero-order correlations, the diagonal values are the SDs.",less-than,"sample_size, p_value, test_statistic","Sample size
